reasons.code_quality: Exemplary idiomatic FastAPI/SQLAlchemy 2.0 code. Uses the recommended `Annotated` pattern for dependency injection (`DbSession` alias), `db.get()` instead of legacy `query().filter()`, Pydantic v2 `model_config` with `from_attributes`, and clean router prefix/tags. Separation of concerns is proper across schemas, routes, and main. Minor deduction: the `DbSession` type alias, while valid, slightly obscures the dependency at the call site for readers unfamiliar with the pattern.
reasons.completeness: Every acceptance criterion is satisfied: `app/schemas/user.py` exists with all four required fields and correct types; `app/routes/users.py` has a `router` variable, uses `Depends(get_db)` (via Annotated), queries `User` from `app.models.user`, returns 200 on hit and 404 with the exact required detail string on miss; router is registered in `app/main.py`; `tests/test_users.py` tests the 404 path with `TestClient`. No migrations, no unauthorized endpoints, no auth added.
reasons.edge_cases: The required error path (404 for missing user) is correctly handled and tested. FastAPI implicitly provides 422 validation for non-integer `user_id` values, but this is not explicitly tested. No coverage of boundary inputs (user_id=0, negative IDs), and the test suite has only one test case. Given the task scope only mandated the 404 case this is acceptable, but nothing beyond the minimum was exercised.
reasons.security: Uses SQLAlchemy ORM `db.get()` throughout — no raw SQL, no injection surface. `user_id` is typed as `int` so FastAPI rejects non-integer inputs at the framework level before the handler runs. No secrets in new files; the default dev secret in `settings.py` is pre-existing seed code with an explicit warning comment and is not part of this submission. No auth bypass risk given the scope explicitly excludes authentication.

overall_notes: A clean, near-reference-quality submission. Fully meets the spec with modern idiomatic patterns (Annotated DI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 Session.get, Pydantic v2 model_config). The only realistic improvement would be adding explicit tests for the 422 path and perhaps a model_config docstring, but neither was required by the task.
